title: Surface Area of a Rectangular Prism

steps.instruction: A rectangular prism has 6 faces: 3 pairs of identical rectangles.
steps.work: Dimensions: l = 6 cm, w = 4 cm, h = 3 cm.
steps.explanation: Each pair of opposite faces has the same area.

steps.instruction: Calculate: top/bottom = l×w, front/back = l×h, left/right = w×h.
steps.explanation: These are the areas of one face from each pair.
Multiply by 2 because each rectangle appears twice.

steps.instruction: Imagine unfolding a triangular prism into its net: 2 triangles and 3 rectangles.
steps.work: Triangle: base 6 cm, height 4 cm. Three rectangles with widths 6, 5, 5 cm and all height 10 cm.
steps.explanation: A net shows all faces laid flat.

steps.instruction: Find the area of each face.
steps.explanation: There are two identical triangular faces and three rectangular faces.
The total surface area is the sum of all faces in the net.
